_description: BioMedAgent self-evolving memory log. Each entry records a successful analysis pattern — tools, workflow, code, and performance metrics. Claude reads this before starting new analyses to reuse proven approaches and avoid past failures.
_usage: Before Phase 1 Step 4 (ToolScorer), search this log for entries matching the current task_category and task_subclass. If a match exists, use its tool_scores, workflow_stages, and code_patterns as a starting point instead of scoring from scratch.

entries.question_signature: binary classification with train/test split, feature selection, multiple classifiers, metrics
workflow_stages: Load data and identify target column
Encode categoricals with LabelEncoder, fill NaN with median
Feature selection with SelectKBest(f_classif, k=min(10, n_features)) → save feature_selected.csv
Train 3 classifiers: RandomForest(n_jobs=-1), SVC(max_iter=500, probability=True), LogisticRegression(max_iter=500)
Compute accuracy + f1 per classifier → classification_results.csv
ROC curve for best classifier → roc_curve.png

code_patterns.performance_critical: Limit to 3 classifiers. Always n_jobs=-1 on RF. Always max_iter=500 on SVM/LR. StandardScaler before SVM/LR. Save after EACH model.
code_patterns.target_detection: Look for 'Target', 'target', 'label', 'class' column. Fall back to last column.

failure_history.what_failed: Running 5+ classifiers including AdaBoost and GradientBoosting
failure_history.why: Timeout at 120s — too many models on large dataset

entries.question_signature: regression with train/test split, feature selection, multiple regressors, MSE/R2 metrics
workflow_stages: Load data and identify target column
Encode categoricals, fill NaN with median
Feature selection with SelectKBest(f_regression) → save feature_selected.csv
Compute RMSE, R2, MAE per regressor → regression_results.csv
Scatter plot actual vs predicted for best model → regression_plot.png

code_patterns.performance_critical: Limit to 3 regressors. SVR(max_iter=500) prevents hang. StandardScaler required for SVR.
code_patterns.metric_computation: Use np.sqrt(mean_squared_error()) for RMSE, not MSE directly

failure_history.what_failed: Running 6 regressors including Ridge, Lasso, AdaBoostRegressor

entries.question_signature: clustering with multiple algorithms, silhouette scores, visualization
workflow_stages: Load data, select numeric columns, encode categoricals
DBSCAN with auto-eps from NearestNeighbors → save dbscan_result.csv
PCA(n_components=2) → scatter plot colored by cluster → clustering_visualization.png

code_patterns.n_clusters_detection: Parse 'N classes' or 'N groups' from question. Default to 3.
code_patterns.dbscan_eps: Use NearestNeighbors(n_neighbors=5), eps = 90th percentile of k-distances
code_patterns.silhouette_guard: Check n_clusters > 1 before computing silhouette. DBSCAN with 1 cluster → silhouette = -1

entries.question_signature: frequent itemset mining, association rules from transaction data
workflow_stages: Load data, identify item column and transaction/member ID column
Group by transaction ID → list of items per transaction

code_patterns.library: mlxtend — install with pip if missing
code_patterns.column_detection: Item column: look for 'item', 'description', 'product'. Transaction column: look for 'member', 'transaction', 'id'.
code_patterns.frozenset_handling: Convert frozenset to sorted comma-separated string before saving to CSV

entries.question_signature: image segmentation with U-Net on archived image data
workflow_stages: If images found: load, resize to 64x64, normalize
Forward pass → threshold at 0.5 → save segmentation_result.png

code_patterns.archive_handling: ALWAYS extract .tar.gz BEFORE any other processing. Use tarfile.open(f, 'r:gz')
code_patterns.gpu_fallback: If no GPU: reduce to 2 epochs, batch_size=1, image_size=64x64

failure_history.why: Code generator treated archive as tabular data
failure_history.fix: Must detect .tar.gz/.tgz inputs and extract first, before any pd.read_csv

entries.question_signature: QQ plot with grouping by categorical column, normality assessment
workflow_stages: Load data with correct separator (\t for .tsv)
Identify grouping column from question text (Genotype, Group2, Sex, etc.)
For each group: scipy.stats.probplot(data, dist='norm', plot=ax)
Subplots: 1 per group, side by side

code_patterns.group_column_detection: Search question for column names that appear in df.columns. Check for 'group', 'genotype', 'type', 'sex', 'treatment'.
code_patterns.subplot_layout: fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, len(groups)). Handle single group: axes = [axes]

entries.question_signature: cross-tabulation / contingency table with proportions from frequency data
workflow_stages: Identify row variable, column variable, and frequency column from question

code_patterns.routing_critical: Trigger on 'cross-tabulation', 'crosstab', 'contingency table', 'proportions', 'frequency table' — check BOTH question AND milestones for these keywords
code_patterns.weighted_crosstab: If data has a Freq/Count column, use pd.crosstab(values=df['Freq'], aggfunc='sum')

failure_history.what_failed: Question didn't contain 'crosstab' keyword — only the milestone did
failure_history.why: Routing only checked question text, not milestones
failure_history.fix: Check combined text (question + milestones) for routing keywords

entries.question_signature: Cox proportional hazards regression with forest plot
workflow_stages: Identify time column ('time', 'survival_time') and event column ('status', 'event')
Select numeric columns, encode categoricals with LabelEncoder

code_patterns.library: lifelines — install with pip if missing
code_patterns.forest_plot: cph.plot(ax=ax) draws the forest plot automatically. Add ax.axvline(x=0) for reference line.

entries.question_signature: area chart/plot grouped by categorical column
workflow_stages: Identify group column and numeric value column
For each group: ax.fill_between() + ax.plot() for line overlay

entries.question_signature: circular pie chart from labels and values columns
workflow_stages: Identify label column ('labels', 'category', 'name') and value column ('values', 'count', 'freq')

code_patterns.autopct: Always include autopct='%1.1f%%' for percentage display

entries.question_signature: kernel density estimation plot grouped by categorical column
workflow_stages: Identify value column ('Value', first numeric) and group column ('Group', 'Group2')
Check milestone for output format (JPG vs PNG)

code_patterns.format_detection: If milestones mention 'jpg format', save as .jpg. If 'png format', save as .png. Default: .png
code_patterns.kde: Use DataFrame.plot.kde() — simpler than seaborn.kdeplot for basic density

entries.question_signature: z-score deviation bar chart sorted descending
workflow_stages: Identify numeric column (often 'mpg' or first numeric)
Color: green if z >= 0, red if z < 0
Y-tick labels from first text column (car name, etc.)

code_patterns.label_column: Use first non-numeric column for y-axis labels. Truncate long labels.
code_patterns.figure_height: max(8, len(df)*0.2) to scale with data size

entries.question_signature: Kaplan-Meier survival curves with risk tables and log-rank test
workflow_stages: Install R packages if missing: survival, survminer
Load data in R: read.csv() or read.delim()
Identify time column, event column, and grouping variable
Fit survival model: survfit(Surv(time, status) ~ group, data=df)
Generate KM plot with risk table: ggsurvplot(fit, pval=TRUE, risk.table=TRUE)
Save as PDF for publication quality: pdf('survival_plot.pdf')
Export survival summary to CSV: write.csv(surv_summary(fit))
If Python fallback needed: use lifelines.KaplanMeierFitter

code_patterns.r_preferred_because: survminer produces publication-ready KM plots with integrated risk tables, confidence bands, p-values, and median survival lines — lifelines cannot match this
code_patterns.python_fallback: lifelines.KaplanMeierFitter + lifelines.statistics.logrank_test — functional but not publication quality
code_patterns.data_interchange: Save CSV from Python, read in R, save results as CSV, pick up in Python
code_patterns.output_format: Always PDF for R survival plots (vector graphics, publication standard)

entries.question_signature: RNA-seq differential gene expression with DESeq2 or edgeR
workflow_stages: Load count matrix and sample metadata
Generate volcano plot: ggplot with log2FC vs -log10(padj)
Optional: downstream pathway enrichment with clusterProfiler

code_patterns.r_preferred_because: DESeq2 uses a negative binomial model with shrinkage estimators specifically designed for RNA-seq count data. Python pydeseq2 exists but is less mature and lacks the full feature set.
code_patterns.python_fallback: pydeseq2 for basic DEG, or scanpy.tl.rank_genes_groups for single-cell
code_patterns.count_matrix_format: Genes as rows, samples as columns. Integer counts, not normalized.

failure_history.why: Bioconductor packages compile from source, can take 5+ minutes
failure_history.fix: Set timeout=600 for Bioconductor installs. Fall back to pydeseq2 if install fails.

entries.question_signature: CEL file processing and normalization with affy or oligo
workflow_stages: Install packages: affy (or oligo for newer arrays), limma
Optional: quality control plots (boxplot, MA plot, density)

code_patterns.r_preferred_because: There is NO Python equivalent for CEL file processing. affy/oligo are the only tools that correctly handle Affymetrix probe-level data with RMA/MAS5 normalization.
code_patterns.python_fallback: NONE for CEL files. Must use R.
code_patterns.platform_annotation: Need GPL annotation package matching the array platform (e.g., hgu133plus2.db)
code_patterns.memory_note: Large CEL datasets (>100 arrays) need 8+ GB RAM

workflow_stages: Install clusterProfiler + organism annotation db (org.Hs.eg.db for human)
Load gene list (from DEG results or user input)
Convert gene IDs if needed: bitr() for ID conversion
Run GO enrichment: enrichGO(gene, OrgDb, keyType, ont='BP')
Save results to CSV and plots to PDF

code_patterns.r_preferred_because: clusterProfiler has built-in access to GO, KEGG, Reactome, MSigDB databases and produces publication-quality enrichment plots. gseapy works but has fewer built-in databases.
code_patterns.python_fallback: gseapy.enrichr() or gseapy.gsea() — functional but fewer plot options
code_patterns.gene_id_types: ENSEMBL, ENTREZID, SYMBOL — use bitr() to convert between them

workflow_stages: Install WGCNA (large dependency tree, allow 10+ min install)
Load normalized expression matrix (genes x samples)
Check for outlier samples: hclust + cutreeStatic

code_patterns.r_preferred_because: WGCNA has NO Python equivalent of comparable quality. PyWGCNA exists but is experimental.
code_patterns.python_fallback: NONE that matches WGCNA functionality. Use R.
code_patterns.memory_critical: WGCNA needs lots of RAM for large gene sets. Use blockwiseModules() for >10k genes.
code_patterns.soft_threshold: Typical range 6-12 for signed networks. Use pickSoftThreshold() to find optimal.

failure_history.why: Large number of compiled dependencies (Rcpp, RcppArmadillo, etc.)
failure_history.fix: Set timeout=900 for WGCNA install. Warn user it will take time.
